calendar.matters:
SMALL CLAIMS APPEAL NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE
Off Calendar Jul-10-2024 Set For Trial On Jul-10-24 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 305

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-09-2024 Continued To Jul-10-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206

SMALL CLAIM APPEALS
Off Calendar Jul-18-2024 Judgment Entered
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:

Joseph F. Zellmer, esq for Appellant/Defendant, who is also present Payam Behradnia DMD Dental Group Inc. represented by Payam Behradnia, CEO.
Michele Ellson, esq for Plaintiff/Respondent Erica Casey Seldon who is also present.

Parties gave each other courtesy copies of their exhibits in the Courtroom and reviewed prior to the hearing.

The cause comes on regularly for Trial De Novo. All parties are placed under oath.

Mr. Zellmer for Appellant presents a Motion to Bifurcate, but first asks if Plaintiff exhibit #2 will be stipulated to by Plaintiff
Ms. Ellson for Plaintiff stipulates Plaintiff's Exhibit #2 can be placed into evidence.

The Trial Brief Motion to Bifurcate for Trail on Statute of Limitations is filed by the Clerek as per directive of the Court.

Mr. Zellmer presents argument for his filed motion.
Ms. Ellson gives response
Mr. Zellmer gives reply

The Court Denies the Motion to Bifurcate and to dismiss.

Ms. Ellson calls witness Ms. Seldon for Direct Examination
and enters the following Exhibits:
#16 Ins policy for replacement of crown
#01 Amount for the original crown. Account History
#08 Treatment notes
#09 Statement from the Doctor

The Court asks Ms. Ellson which exhibits will be entered.
Ms. Ellson offers Exhibits 1# through 16#
Mr. Zellmer objects
Then Court will review the and decide on the objection later.

Mr. Zellmer commences Cross Examination on the witness, and offers the following exhibits:
#01 Email String of the Plaintiff and the Defendant's office.
#02 Dental Treatment consent form
#05 Dental Treatment consent form
#06 Consent for final cementation for final fix.
#07 Collection of documents and photos

11:16 The court takes a 10 min recess.

Mr. Zellmer continues with Cross Examination and offers exhibit:
#04 Treatment plan
Ms. Ellson continues with Re Direct and reviews Defendant's exhibits previously presented: #2,#5,#6
Mr. Zellmer continues with Cross Examination
Ms. Ellson continues with Re Direct
The Witness is thanked and excused.

The Court reviews the Plaintiff's exhibits offered into evidence
Ms. Ellson offers all her exhibits #1- #16
Mr. Zeller has no objections, and offers to admit Defendant's exhibits #1 - #7
Ms. Ellson has no objections.

Ms. Ellson evokes evidence code 776 and calls witness Dr. Payam Behradnia.
Ms. Ellson commences Cross Examination on the witness.
Mr. Zellmer commences with Direct Examination.

Mr. Zeller presents Oral Motion for Non-Suit and presents arguments.
Ms. Ellson responds and cites previously admitted exhibit #14
The Court denies the oral motion

12:20 The Court takes its Lunch Break to resume at 1:30pm.

01:34 Mr. Zeller commences Direct examination
Ms. Ellson commences Cross Examination on the witness.
Mr. Zellmer continues with the Re-direct Examination.
The Court asks questions clarifying the sequence of events.
The Court thanks and excuses the witness.

Ms. Ellson recalls plaintiff/witness Ms. Selden

Parties submit

Ms. Ellson for the Plaintiff gives closing summation.
Mr. Zeller asks the Court if his motion was denied without prejudice
The Court affirms his request.
Mr. Zeller requests the Court to re-consider his motion to dismiss.
The Court will consider his motion.

At the conclusion of this case the Court takes this case under submission.

Appellant and Respondent exhibits retained.
Judge: Mary E. Wiss; Clerk: R. Sahatjian. Not reported. (D305)
